headline: BK Connectedness estimates are exposed in full so you can read the result and reproduce it manually.

sections.title: What is shown on this page
items: Supporting tables included: band_total_connectedness, short_connectedness_matrix, short_directional_spillovers, medium_connectedness_matrix, medium_directional_spillovers, long_connectedness_matrix, long_directional_spillovers.
Figures included: BK frequency connectedness, BK band connectedness summary.

sections.title: How to read the main result
items: Each band isolates spillovers at a different horizon segment; compare short-, medium-, and long-run connectedness rather than collapsing everything into one number.
The band-total connectedness chart summarizes how much spillover intensity sits in each frequency range.
Band-specific matrices remain normalized within band, so they are for structural comparison rather than direct level comparison across bands.

sections.title: What a normal paper should report
items: A summary table of total connectedness by frequency band.
A figure showing how connectedness is distributed across short, medium, and long horizons.

sections.title: Manual replication focus
items: Rebuild the VAR with the same lag order before moving to the frequency decomposition.
Use the documented short and medium horizon cutoffs exactly when reconstructing the bands.
Check each band's total connectedness against the band summary chart and table.
